title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: c001 lacks citation to controlling primary authorities (e.g., FLSA case law/economic‑realities decisions, IRS common‑law tests/guidance, state precedents such as Borello/Dynamex); supporting_sources are non‑identifying placeholders preventing verification.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: The claim’s phrasing that “source quality determines the controlling test” conflates persuasive weight with legal control; controlling tests are set by jurisdictional primary law, not source quality alone.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: c001: Vague reliance on “high-authority sources” without citing controlling primary authorities (statutes, regulations, Supreme Court/state supreme court or binding appellate decisions, IRS/DOL guidance) — source quality is asserted but not demonstrated.

title: Resolve knowledge gap
question: Critic follow-up: c001: Fails to address whether and when federal doctrines (e.g., IRS common‑law test vs. DOL economic‑realities) are in conflict and which preemption or supremacy rules determine the controlling standard.
